extraction.one_line_summary: EWAN Caleb won stage 8 into Pesaro by waiting until ACKERMANN Pascal's early final-corner launch faded, then countering with 200m remaining.
tactical_patterns: CICCONE Giulio aggressively hunted KOM points at every opportunity, suggesting King of Mountains jersey defense takes priority over energy conservation.
EWAN's team isolated him early but his sprint acceleration reserves compensated, showing lead-out collapse doesn't eliminate top sprinters on technical finishes.
ACKERMANN Pascal launched from final corner 250m out despite full team support still available, revealing overeagerness after multi-day winless streak.
GC teams repositioned leaders on descent 25km from finish despite flat finale, indicating concern about crashes overrides energy conservation logic.

rider_signals.EWAN Caleb: positive: converted isolation into victory by timing launch perfectly after ACKERMANN faded, demonstrating elite acceleration compensates for lead-out absence
rider_signals.ACKERMANN Pascal: negative: initiated sprint from final corner with full team still present, wasted advantage by going 250m out when rivals were conserving
rider_signals.VIVIANI Elia: neutral: refused to follow own lead-out train repeatedly, positioning behind EWAN instead suggests doubt in CONSONNI/SÉNÉCHAL pace judgment
rider_signals.CICCONE Giulio: positive: attacked for KOM points multiple times despite peloton chase, showing jersey defense aggression outweighs breakaway cooperation norms
rider_signals.ROGLIČ Primož: neutral: positioned on second wheel with 25km remaining on flat roads, indicating extreme crash caution despite no GC threat materializing
